thesis: Rocket Lab is the most credible publicly traded challenger to SpaceX in the commercial launch market. Its vertically integrated model — covering reusable small-lift Electron launches, satellite components, spacecraft manufacturing, and the upcoming medium-lift Neutron rocket — creates a durable competitive position in the expanding space economy. Q1 2026 revenue of $200.3M grew 63.5% YoY, with backlog exceeding $2.2B (up 108% YoY), while $2B+ in total liquidity funds the Neutron development program without near-term dilution. The stock has pulled back to $76.20 from a $151.00 52-week high, partly due to broad space-sector selling after the Blue Origin test failure and investor focus on a potential SpaceX IPO. This reset improves the risk/reward for speculative investors who can tolerate execution risk through H2 2026 and 2027.

drivers: Neutron first flight successful in H2 2026 and commercial bookings accelerate
Golden Dome/Iridium-related awards and SDA/DoD Tranche wins add $200M+ in high-margin revenue
Space Systems margins expand to 50%+ as satellite bus production scales
EBITDA inflection turns positive by late 2026/early 2027
Nasdaq-100 inclusion and improving sentiment drive multiple expansion

base_case.assumptions: Neutron first flight occurs in late 2026/H1 2027 with minor delays; commercial ramp begins H1 2027. Steady government contract wins sustain $250M+ annual launch revenue. Space Systems grows 40%+ YoY with modest margin expansion. Company reaches EBITDA breakeven by mid-2027. Stock trades at 8–10x forward sales, reflecting solid execution but no multiple expansion.

risks: Neutron first flight delayed into 2027 or experiences anomaly
Government contract awards disappoint relative to expectations
Cash burn exceeds plan, forcing dilutive capital raise
Competition from SpaceX and emerging launch providers intensifies
Space Systems growth decelerates due to customer concentration or supply chain issues
Macro risk-off environment compresses growth stock multiples

catalysts.event: Neutron first flight — binary event validating medium-lift strategy and opening the constellation deployment market
Nasdaq-100 inclusion — already added; passive inflows increase institutional ownership and liquidity
Q2 2026 earnings — backlog conversion and margin progress; guidance for revenue $225M–$240M and Adjusted EBITDA loss $20M–$26M
SDA/DoD contract awards — high-margin government revenue expansion and Tranche wins
Electron cadence increase and Archimedes engine test campaign — higher launch frequency improves unit economics
Space Systems margin expansion — satellite bus production scaling and component sales to third parties

key_risks: Neutron development risk — medium-lift rocket development is technically complex and capital intensive; delays or anomaly would hurt sentiment
Government dependency — significant revenue from DoD/SDA; budget cuts or contract delays would hurt
Competition — SpaceX dominates launch; other startups (Relativity, ABL) emerging
Execution risk — scaling manufacturing for both launch and space systems is operationally complex
Macro / valuation risk — high-growth stock vulnerable to rate shocks and risk-off sentiment; recent selling after Blue Origin test failure and SpaceX IPO focus
Cash burn — Neutron development and scaling may consume cash faster than expected, potentially requiring dilutive financing
